overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly specialized professional presence across academic-focused social networks, including LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon. His content effectively targets researchers and data scientists, focusing on computational social science, disinformation, and tool releases. While LinkedIn provides the largest raw audience and absolute reach, Bluesky emerges as the most efficient channel for community interaction, driving the highest relative engagement rates.

contentThemes.description: Research updates, paper publications, and presentations analyzing coordinated link sharing and information operations.
Job openings, postdoctoral roles, and PhD opportunities within his research team.
Technical releases and algorithmic updates for specialized research tools like CooRnet.

recommendations: Cross-promote major software updates (such as CooRnet v2.0) across LinkedIn and Bluesky, as they are currently siloed on Mastodon despite being highly relevant to the broader audience.
Leverage Bluesky's highly active academic community by continuing to post early-stage research questions and student recruitment opportunities.
Utilize more visual elements, such as data visualizations, tool screenshots, or paper abstracts, to further increase engagement on LinkedIn professional posts.

platformInsights.bluesky: Acts as the most highly engaged niche community, yielding an average engagement rate of 6.14%. Highly effective for sharing research papers and recruitment opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: Serves as a robust channel for technical updates and open-source tool announcements (average 4.55% engagement), appealing to a highly specialized and technically-minded audience.
platformInsights.linkedin: Functions as the primary professional hub with the largest audience (1,850 followers). It drives the highest absolute engagement numbers (e.g., 107 interactions on the grant announcement), making it critical for high-impact professional milestones.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
